Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I have tested this patch and it does work. I started to think about everyone asking questions and when I took a closer look at the code I realized its just a mess. The reason for all the comments is everyone is confused. So I started to play around with the code to see if it can be cleaned up.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(6 comments)

Definitely one leak in the error path that needs to be fixed.

While this will definitely fix the problem on client-only builds, it isn't clear to me how it fixes the problem of mounting a client filesystem if the server RPM was installed (e.g. if the site wants to keep the same kernel everywhere and uses the server RPMs on the clients)?

I was thinking of just biting the bullet and having the lustre.ko module loaded on the server nodes, but this also drags in lov.ko, mdc.ko, etc. even if they are not needed. Unfortunately I don't have much better to offer than possibly splitting up the fstype between client and server, which would help simplify a bunch of this code, but complicates the upgrade process and documentation significantly.

Patch Set 1:

The issue of client mounts done in rpms installed from server builds is addressed  by the new added code in the client only mount execution path in lustre_fill_super() in obd_mount.c.  symbol_request(force_client_load) forces the load of lustre.ko if it isn't already loaded.

parents.subject: LU-4970 tests: turn up debugging for sanity-lfsck/14

commit.subject: LU-4800 obdclass: runtime load lustre client when needed
commit.message:
LU-4800 obdclass: runtime load lustre client when needed

Manually force loading of lustre.ko during client
mounts with request_module for cases where
autoloading doesn't happen.

Signed-off-by: Bob Glossman <bob.glossman@intel.com>
Change-Id: I807fc5cd04cf5a97d9c131208b7b13037dc10ee6
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10587
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: James Simmons <uja.ornl@gmail.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Yang Sheng <yang.sheng@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
